violations.text: Crepe batter observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Crepe batter was placed in freezer to rapidly chill, and will be kept on ice going forward per PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Food observed stored on the floor beside the refrigerator. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Single service items observed stored on the floor beside the refrigerator. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Single service items observed being reused for food preparation and storage. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerators, freezer, and prep unit have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
The kitchen counter is in poor repair, with torn aluminum tape. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (blender bowls) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Blender bowls are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2024

The cutting boards in the cutting board are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
The 3 bay sink was not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerator have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2024
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. It is an accordian-style door, and was open at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2024

The board in poor repair in the crepe-making area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2024
The cupboard doors are in poor repair, with one missing and other falling off the hinges. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

The board alongside the crepe making area is in poor repair, with the laminate broken and missing in places. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
Establishment is lacking an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
Employee observed filling coffee pot in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
Food container was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer must be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Batter was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC put batter in ice bath. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Frozen strawberries observed thawing on the floor near the prep unit fan vent. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Utensil were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Spatulas observed stored on an unclean surface beneath crepe makers. Utensils must be stored in a clean dry location, or in water held at 135 degrees F or above. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
Three bay sink was not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

The cutting boards on the prep unit are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

Wood is used in the construction of crepe station (side ledge). Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the can opener have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2024
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
